Implements functions for creating and managing action menu hierarchies in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions for managing an action menu system, which is a hierarchical structure of menu items and levels. It provides functionality to create and manipulate `ActionMenuLevel` and `ActionMenuItem` objects. The code includes functions to create a menu level with a specified maximum number of items, set the display mode of a level, and add action items or child levels to a menu level. Each action item can have a label, a callback function for performing an action, and associated action data.

The code also includes functions to retrieve the label and action data of an action menu item. The [`action_menu_hierarchy_destroy`](<#action_menu_hierarchy_destroy>) function, along with the helper function [`prv_cleanup_helper`](<#prv_cleanup_helper>), is used to recursively clean up and free the memory associated with the entire menu hierarchy. The code uses memory allocation functions from the `applib` and `kernel` libraries to manage dynamic memory for the menu structures. This code is intended to be part of a larger application where action menus are used to organize and execute user actions in a structured manner.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `action_menu_hierarchy.h`
- `action_menu_window_private.h`
- `applib/applib_malloc.auto.h`
- `kernel/pbl_malloc.h`


# Functions

---
### action\_menu\_item\_get\_label<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_item_get_label}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L25>)

Retrieves the label of a given `ActionMenuItem`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `item`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuItem` from which to get the label.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `item` is `NULL`.
    - If `item` is `NULL`, return `NULL`.
    - If `item` is not `NULL`, return the `label` field of `item` cast to a `char *`.
- **Output**: A pointer to a `char` string representing the label of the `ActionMenuItem`, or `NULL` if `item` is `NULL`.


---
### action\_menu\_item\_get\_action\_data<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_item_get_action_data}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L32>)

Retrieves the `action_data` from an `ActionMenuItem` if it is a leaf node.
- **Inputs**:
    - `item`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuItem` structure from which to retrieve the `action_data`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `item` is `NULL` or if `item->is_leaf` is `false`.
    - If either condition is true, return `NULL`.
    - If both conditions are false, return the `action_data` from the `item`.
- **Output**: A pointer to the `action_data` of the `ActionMenuItem`, or `NULL` if the item is `NULL` or not a leaf.


---
### action\_menu\_level\_create<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_level_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L42>)

Allocates and initializes an `ActionMenuLevel` structure with a specified maximum number of items.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``max_items``: The maximum number of items that the `ActionMenuLevel` can contain.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call `applib_malloc` to allocate memory for an `ActionMenuLevel` structure and its items, using the size of `ActionMenuLevel` and `max_items` multiplied by the size of `ActionMenuItem`.
    - Check if the memory allocation was successful; if not, return `NULL`.
    - Initialize the `ActionMenuLevel` structure with `max_items` and set `display_mode` to `ActionMenuLevelDisplayModeWide`.
    - Return the pointer to the newly created `ActionMenuLevel` structure.
- **Output**: A pointer to the newly created `ActionMenuLevel` structure, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.


---
### action\_menu\_level\_set\_display\_mode<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_level_set_display_mode}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L55>)

Sets the display mode of a given `ActionMenuLevel`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `level`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLevel` structure whose display mode you want to set.
    - `display_mode`: The `ActionMenuLevelDisplayMode` value to set for the `level`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `level` is not NULL; if it is NULL, return immediately without making changes.
    - Set the `display_mode` field of the `level` to the provided `display_mode`.
- **Output**: No output is returned.


---
### action\_menu\_level\_add\_action<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_level_add_action}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L61>)

Adds an action item to a specified action menu level if conditions are met.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``level``: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLevel` structure where the action item will be added.
    - ``label``: A string representing the label of the action item.
    - ``cb``: A callback function of type `ActionMenuPerformActionCb` that will be executed when the action is performed.
    - ``action_data``: A pointer to any data associated with the action item.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `level`, `label`, and `cb` are not NULL and if `level->num_items` is less than `level->max_items`; if any condition fails, return NULL.
    - Create a pointer `item` to the next available `ActionMenuItem` in the `level->items` array.
    - Initialize the `item` with the provided `label`, `cb`, and `action_data`.
    - Increment `level->num_items` to reflect the addition of the new item.
    - Return the pointer to the newly added `ActionMenuItem`.
- **Output**: A pointer to the newly added `ActionMenuItem`, or NULL if the addition fails.


---
### action\_menu\_level\_add\_child<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_level_add_child}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L80>)

Adds a child `ActionMenuLevel` to a parent `ActionMenuLevel` with a specified label.
- **Inputs**:
    - `level`: A pointer to the parent `ActionMenuLevel` to which the child will be added.
    - `child`: A pointer to the child `ActionMenuLevel` that will be added to the parent.
    - `label`: A string representing the label for the new child item.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `level`, `child`, or `label` is NULL or if the number of items in `level` has reached its maximum; if any condition is true, return NULL.
    - Set the `parent_level` of `child` to `level`.
    - Create a new `ActionMenuItem` at the next available index in `level->items`.
    - Initialize the new `ActionMenuItem` with the provided `label` and set its `next_level` to `child`.
    - Increment the `num_items` count of `level`.
    - Return a pointer to the newly created `ActionMenuItem`.
- **Output**: A pointer to the newly created `ActionMenuItem`, or NULL if the operation fails.


---
### prv\_cleanup\_helper<!-- {{#callable:prv_cleanup_helper}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L103>)

Recursively traverses and cleans up an `ActionMenuLevel` hierarchy, applying a callback to each item and freeing memory.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``level``: A pointer to the `ActionMenuLevel` structure to clean up.
    - ``each_cb``: A callback function to apply to each `ActionMenuItem` during traversal.
    - ``context``: A pointer to user-defined data passed to the callback function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Iterates over each item in the `level` using a for loop.
    - Checks if an item is not a leaf and has a `next_level`; if true, calls `prv_cleanup_helper` recursively on `next_level`.
    - If `each_cb` is not NULL, calls `each_cb` with the current item and `context`.
    - Frees the memory allocated for the `level` using `applib_free`.
- **Output**: No return value; performs cleanup and memory deallocation.


---
### action\_menu\_hierarchy\_destroy<!-- {{#callable:action_menu_hierarchy_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.c#L119>)

Destroys an action menu hierarchy starting from the root level, optionally applying a callback to each item.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``root``: A pointer to the root `ActionMenuLevel` of the hierarchy to destroy.
    - ``each_cb``: A callback function of type `ActionMenuEachItemCb` to apply to each item in the hierarchy, or `NULL` if no callback is needed.
    - ``context``: A pointer to user-defined data to pass to the callback function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `root` is not `NULL`.
    - If `root` is not `NULL`, call [`prv_cleanup_helper`](<#prv_cleanup_helper>) with `root`, `each_cb`, and `context` as arguments.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_cleanup_helper`](<#prv_cleanup_helper>)
